verifier: fresh cross-agent verifier R391 (claude-opus-4-7, no prior context)
rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL
candidate_summary: BAGH-POOL: hierarchical multi-scale transformer with per-scale pooling P_s plus an explicit intra-pool variance penalty term lambda_s * Var(F_s) imposing progressive smoothness at deeper scales.

results.title: PINN Loss Regularization Variance-Based Term (2024/12)
results.rationale: Variance-based regularization loss combined with MSE for neural networks. Functionally overlaps with candidate's lambda*Var(F_s) smoothness penalty even though it lacks per-scale hierarchical pool structure. Crosses sem and func 0.7.

results.title: Multi-scale and Multi-pooling Sparse Filtering
results.rationale: Multi-scale + multi-pooling feature fusion with sparse-filtering regularization. Combines hierarchical pooling with a regularization objective on pooled features — same mechanism cluster as BAGH-POOL. Crosses sem and func 0.7.

results.title: Learning Smooth Neural Functions via Lipschitz Regularization (2022)
results.rationale: Explicit smoothness loss via Jacobian/Lipschitz norm — exactly the 'progressive smoothness as explicit objective' that the candidate frames as cooling. Same effect (force smoother features via added loss term), differs only in penalty form (Lipschitz vs intra-pool variance). At/above the 0.7 threshold.

results.title: Variance Inflation Regularization in Autoencoders
results.rationale: Explicit variance regularization on feature space within deep representation learning. Mechanism is a variance-penalty term on layer features — the core of candidate's lambda*Var(F_s). Crosses both thresholds.

results.title: Multi-scale Hierarchical ViT Cascaded Attention Decoding
results.rationale: Hierarchical multi-scale ViT with cascaded decoding — candidate explicitly admits this prior is similar but claims novelty via added variance penalty. Functional pool-cascade twin minus the regularizer. Crosses 0.7 on both.

results.title: EfficientViT Multi-Scale Linear Attention
results.rationale: Multi-scale linear-attention for efficiency, no explicit smoothness/variance penalty term. Below 0.7 thresholds on the smoothness-objective axis.

results.title: Grouped Multi-Scale ViT GMSA (2025)
results.rationale: Channel-group multi-scale attention; explicitly named in candidate as prior that does NOT include variance penalty. Below thresholds on the variance-regularization axis.

results.title: Mix-layers semantic extraction + Multi-scale Aggregation Transformer
results.rationale: Multi-scale aggregation Transformer with multi-stage feature-mixing LOSS aggregation — explicit multi-scale loss across pooled stages overlaps strongly with candidate's per-scale loss-term scheme. Crosses 0.7 on both.

verdict_sentence: FAIL — at least six of eight retrieved papers (PINN variance loss, Multi-scale Multi-pooling Sparse Filtering, Lipschitz smoothness regularization, Variance Inflation Regularization, Cascaded Attention Decoding, and Mix-layers Multi-scale Aggregation Transformer) independently cross the sem>=0.7 / func>=0.7 thresholds by combining either hierarchical multi-scale pooling with a regularization loss or explicit variance/smoothness penalties on features, so total_hits>=1 and BAGH-POOL is rejected as non-novel.
